criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented _execute_via_deepagents async seam in src/forge/build/test_verification.py; verify_tests calls only that seam. Default seam body raises RuntimeError so unwired calls fail loud. test_calls_execute_seam_not_subprocess_run patches subprocess.run with an AssertionError-raising bomb to prove subprocess.run is never invoked. test_default_seam_raises_runtime_error confirms the unwired behaviour. set_execute_seam() exposed for orchestrator-boot wiring of the real DeepAgents execute tool.
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented _execute_via_deepagents async seam in src/forge/build/test_verification.py; verify_tests calls only that seam. Default seam body raises RuntimeError so unwired calls fail loud. test_calls_execute_seam_not_subprocess_run patches subprocess.run with an AssertionError-raising bomb to prove subprocess.run is never invoked. test_default_seam_raises_runtime_error confirms the unwired behaviour. set_execute_seam() exposed for orchestrator-boot wiring of the real DeepAgents execute tool.

criteria_verification.evidence: verify_tests passes str(worktree_path) as cwd to the execute seam. Pre-execution check raises ValueError if worktree_path.is_absolute() is False so the execute tool's allowlist always sees an absolute path. test_cwd_is_absolute_worktree_path asserts the seam receives the absolute worktree path; test_relative_worktree_path_raises_value_error asserts the boundary check.
criteria_verification.notes: verify_tests passes str(worktree_path) as cwd to the execute seam. Pre-execution check raises ValueError if worktree_path.is_absolute() is False so the execute tool's allowlist always sees an absolute path. test_cwd_is_absolute_worktree_path asserts the seam receives the absolute worktree path; test_relative_worktree_path_raises_value_error asserts the boundary check.

criteria_verification.evidence: passed = exit_code == 0 in verify_tests, with timeout overriding to passed=False. test_exit_code_zero_means_passed and test_exit_code_one_means_failed cover the mapping; the no-tests/collection-error/timeout cases also assert passed=False with non-zero exit codes.
criteria_verification.notes: passed = exit_code == 0 in verify_tests, with timeout overriding to passed=False. test_exit_code_zero_means_passed and test_exit_code_one_means_failed cover the mapping; the no-tests/collection-error/timeout cases also assert passed=False with non-zero exit codes.

criteria_verification.evidence: _parse_summary scans stdout for the pytest summary line via _SUMMARY_LINE_RE plus _PASSED_RE / _FAILED_RE / _ERROR_RE so counts are order-independent. Errors fold into fail_count. Unknown formats yield COUNTS_UNAVAILABLE (-1) and the docstring documents the fallback. test_all_pass_summary_yields_pass_count, test_partial_fail_summary_yields_both_counts, test_no_tests_collected_yields_unavailable_counts, test_collection_error_folds_errors_into_fail_count, and test_unknown_summary_format_yields_unavailable_counts cover the matrix.
criteria_verification.notes: _parse_summary scans stdout for the pytest summary line via _SUMMARY_LINE_RE plus _PASSED_RE / _FAILED_RE / _ERROR_RE so counts are order-independent. Errors fold into fail_count. Unknown formats yield COUNTS_UNAVAILABLE (-1) and the docstring documents the fallback. test_all_pass_summary_yields_pass_count, test_partial_fail_summary_yields_both_counts, test_no_tests_collected_yields_unavailable_counts, test_collection_error_folds_errors_into_fail_count, and test_unknown_summary_format_yields_unavailable_counts cover the matrix.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Extract failing test identifiers (the `FAILED tests/...` lines)
criteria_verification.evidence: _extract_failing_tests scans stdout for ^FAILED\s+(\S+) and preserves order while deduplicating (pytest emits FAILED lines both inline and in the short-test-summary section). test_partial_fail_summary_yields_both_counts asserts the three expected identifiers in order; test_failed_lines_are_deduplicated asserts a re-emitted FAILED line is collapsed to a single entry.
criteria_verification.notes: _extract_failing_tests scans stdout for ^FAILED\s+(\S+) and preserves order while deduplicating (pytest emits FAILED lines both inline and in the short-test-summary section). test_partial_fail_summary_yields_both_counts asserts the three expected identifiers in order; test_failed_lines_are_deduplicated asserts a re-emitted FAILED line is collapsed to a single entry.

criteria_verification.evidence: _tail(s, n) returns the last n characters of stdout (or the full string if shorter; empty for n<=0). DEFAULT_OUTPUT_TAIL_CHARS=4000 is the default; verify_tests exposes output_tail_chars override. test_output_tail_returns_last_4000_chars_by_default proves the default and that the slice is a suffix of stdout. test_output_tail_chars_is_configurable proves the override path. test_output_tail_returns_full_string_when_shorter proves the under-N case.
criteria_verification.notes: _tail(s, n) returns the last n characters of stdout (or the full string if shorter; empty for n<=0). DEFAULT_OUTPUT_TAIL_CHARS=4000 is the default; verify_tests exposes output_tail_chars override. test_output_tail_returns_last_4000_chars_by_default proves the default and that the slice is a suffix of stdout. test_output_tail_chars_is_configurable proves the override path. test_output_tail_returns_full_string_when_shorter proves the under-N case.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `duration_seconds` parsed from pytest summary; fall back to wall-clock
criteria_verification.evidence: _parse_summary returns the duration captured from the pytest summary line ('in 4.20s' → 4.2). verify_tests prefers the parsed value, falling back to the seam-reported exec_duration, then to time.monotonic() wall-clock. test_duration_parsed_from_pytest_summary asserts the summary wins over the seam value (99.0); test_duration_falls_back_to_seam_value_when_summary_missing asserts the fallback path.
criteria_verification.notes: _parse_summary returns the duration captured from the pytest summary line ('in 4.20s' → 4.2). verify_tests prefers the parsed value, falling back to the seam-reported exec_duration, then to time.monotonic() wall-clock. test_duration_parsed_from_pytest_summary asserts the summary wins over the seam value (99.0); test_duration_falls_back_to_seam_value_when_summary_missing asserts the fallback path.

criteria_verification.evidence: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=600 is the default. _timeout_result builds the canonical timeout shape (passed=False, failing_tests=[TIMEOUT_MARKER='__TIMEOUT__'], pass/fail_count=COUNTS_UNAVAILABLE). Both signalling shapes — seam returns timed_out=True and seam raises TimeoutError — normalise through this helper. test_returns_passed_false_with_timeout_marker, test_timeout_duration_uses_seam_value_when_present, test_timeout_preserves_partial_stdout_in_output_tail, test_timeout_error_normalised_to_timeout_result, test_default_timeout_is_600_seconds, and test_custom_timeout_is_forwarded cover the boundary.
criteria_verification.notes: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=600 is the default. _timeout_result builds the canonical timeout shape (passed=False, failing_tests=[TIMEOUT_MARKER='__TIMEOUT__'], pass/fail_count=COUNTS_UNAVAILABLE). Both signalling shapes — seam returns timed_out=True and seam raises TimeoutError — normalise through this helper. test_returns_passed_false_with_timeout_marker, test_timeout_duration_uses_seam_value_when_present, test_timeout_preserves_partial_stdout_in_output_tail, test_timeout_error_normalised_to_timeout_result, test_default_timeout_is_600_seconds, and test_custom_timeout_is_forwarded cover the boundary.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
criteria_verification.evidence: python3 -m black --check --fast --target-version py311 reports '5 files would be left unchanged' across the new src/forge/build/* and tests/forge/build/* files. python3 -m ruff check reports 'All checks passed!' for the same set. The full forge test suite still passes at 1051 tests.

issues.description: No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.

rationale: All quality gates passed. Independent verification confirmed. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 326 chars provided.
context_used:
## Job-Specific Context

Budget: 1600/5200 tokens

### 🎨 Relevant Patterns
- ENABLES
- IS_CATEGORY_OF
- INVOLVES
- IS_USED_IN_PATTERN

### ⚠️ Warnings
- HAS_SYMPTOM
- IS_IMPLEMENTED_AS
- CAUSES

### 🎭 Role Constraints
- HAS_ROLE_MUST_DO
- MUST_CONSIDER
- PRODUCES

### 🛠️ Implementation Modes
- PITFALL_OF_IMPLEMENTATION_MODE
